name: Technology
description: Tech companies, the internet, gadgets, and cryptocurrency

questions.q: Who co-founded Apple Inc. alongside Steve Jobs in 1976?

source.notes: Wozniak designed the Apple I and Apple II; Ronald Wayne was a short-lived third co-founder.

questions.q: Who founded Microsoft with Paul Allen in 1975?

source.notes: Originally called 'Micro-Soft'.

questions.q: Which South African-born entrepreneur founded both SpaceX and Tesla?

source.notes: Founded SpaceX in 2002; joined Tesla as an early investor and later CEO.

questions.q: What is the name of the first decentralized cryptocurrency, launched in 2009?

source.notes: Launched in January 2009 by the pseudonymous Satoshi Nakamoto.

questions.q: Who founded the online retailer Amazon.com in 1994?

source.notes: Originally started from Bezos's garage in Bellevue, Washington.

questions.q: In Windows applications, what does the keyboard shortcut Ctrl + C do?

source.notes: Ctrl+X = Cut, Ctrl+V = Paste, Ctrl+Z = Undo.

questions.q: Who co-founded Google with Larry Page while the two were Stanford PhD students?

source.notes: Incorporated Google in 1998 out of a garage in Menlo Park.

questions.q: Atari released which iconic arcade tennis video game in 1972?

source.notes: One of the earliest commercially successful video games.

questions.q: Who founded Facebook from his Harvard dorm room in 2004?

source.notes: Co-founded with Eduardo Saverin, Dustin Moskovitz, Chris Hughes, and Andrew McCollum.

questions.q: Bitcoin was launched by an individual or group operating under which pseudonym?

source.notes: True identity has never been confirmed.

questions.q: Reed Hastings co-founded which streaming service in 1997 with Marc Randolph?

source.notes: Started as a DVD-by-mail business in Scotts Valley, California.

questions.q: The hugely popular mobile game 'Angry Birds' was created in which country?

source.notes: Developed by Rovio Entertainment in Espoo, Finland; released 2009.

questions.q: Amazon, which was founded in 1994, is headquartered in which U.S. state?

source.notes: Seattle-based; moved to the South Lake Union neighborhood in 2010.

questions.q: What does the acronym 'BSoD' stand for in the context of Microsoft Windows errors?

source.notes: Occurs after a fatal system error in Windows.

questions.q: Google is owned by which parent holding company, created during a 2015 restructuring?

source.notes: Alphabet Inc. became the parent of Google in October 2015.

questions.q: The protocol underlying Bitcoin caps the total number of bitcoins that can ever exist at approximately what number?

source.notes: Expected to be fully mined around the year 2140.

questions.q: A file with the extension .mp3 contains what type of content?

source.notes: Compressed audio format developed by the Fraunhofer Society.

questions.q: YouTube was founded in 2005 by Chad Hurley, Steve Chen, and which third co-founder?

source.notes: Karim uploaded the first-ever YouTube video, 'Me at the zoo', in April 2005.

questions.q: Which messaging service did Jan Koum and Brian Acton co-found before selling it to Facebook in 2014?

source.notes: Facebook paid approximately $19 billion for WhatsApp.

questions.q: Which Swedish entrepreneur co-founded the music streaming service Spotify in 2006?

source.notes: Co-founded with Martin Lorentzon.

questions.q: Which blockchain platform introduced the concept of programmable 'smart contracts'?

source.notes: Proposed by Vitalik Buterin in 2013; went live in 2015.

questions.q: After leaking classified NSA documents in 2013, whistleblower Edward Snowden ultimately took refuge in which country?

source.notes: Granted asylum; received Russian citizenship in 2022.
